title_en: Tokyo's Shorts Policy Sparks 'Leg Hair Harassment' Debate Amid Heatwave
title_zh: 东京短裤政策引发‘腿毛骚扰’争论 酷暑之下推广遇冷
summary_zh: 东京都知事小池百合子今年四月在“东京清凉商务”活动中允许男性员工穿短裤上班，以应对高温并节约能源。政策实施近四个月后评价两极：部分人享受宽松着装，但女性抱怨被迫看到男同事腿毛，网上出现“腿毛骚扰”一词；调查显示53.5%的人反对穿短裤，女性抵触情绪更明显。男性则因顾虑腿毛而开始寻求激光脱毛。该政策延续了日本自2005年启动的Cool Biz节能传统，当前日本正经历创纪录高温与能源价格飙升，促使民众采取多种降温措施。
reading_passage:
Tokyo Governor Yuriko Koike introduced "Tokyo Cool Biz" in April, hoping to expand on her signature cool down initiative which has now become a widely recognised annual summer tradition.

Almost four months in, the newly allowed shorts in the office policy is getting mixed reviews.

Some commentators enjoy the relaxed rules, helping them work more comfortably. Others say the policy is unfair to women since they are still expected to wear tights when showing a part of their legs. And a few women describe the experience of "leg hair harassment" or a term coined online as "sunehara", referring to the discomfort of being forced to see their colleague's leg hair.

"We want to give people more options in the severe heat, not tell them what to wear. There should be no issue as long as the work attire is not offensive to anyone," Tokyo Metropolitan Government environmental official Noboru Watanabe told the BBC.

A June survey by Gorilla Clinic found 53.5% opposed wearing shorts to work in the summer while 46.5% were in favour of the new recommendation.

For those against shorts in the office the main reason, for both genders, was the concern over body shape and body hair. The clinic says women significantly outnumbered men in their response, indicating that women may be more resistant to seeing a male colleague's bare legs.

In recent years more Japanese workers, notably those working at startups and tech companies, have started wearing casual clothes. But this year, the Tokyo government recommendation has made it more socially acceptable. Still, some men have been feeling self-conscious about their leg hair.

Akifumi Funatsu, the director of Gorilla Clinic, says he has noticed more clients coming to the clinic not just for cosmetic reasons, but as a matter of "social etiquette when wearing shorts". He thinks women believe that "men should have less leg hair", which has prompted them to seek laser hair removal so as not to offend those around them.

Koike first introduced the summer wardrobe shift as Japan's environment minister in 2005. She launched a national energy-saving campaign called "Cool Biz" to encourage company workers to opt for short-sleeve shirts.

Government leaders participated in the initiative, with then Prime Minister Junichiro Koizumi often seen in public without wearing a tie or jacket, helping get the word out.

A few years later following the Tohoku earthquake and tsunami in March 2011, the government launched "Super Cool Biz" which expanded the guidelines to help reduce energy use both at work and at home.

As with other parts of the world, Japan has been experiencing record heat in recent months while grappling with soaring energy prices driven by instability in the Middle East.

Forecasters have warned of extreme heat this summer, with the Japan Meteorological Agency trying to raise public awareness by announcing the word "kokusho-bi", which means "brutally hot day" when the mercury exceeds 40C.

The latest weekly data shows seven people died in Japan from heatstroke and 4,580 people were admitted to hospitals in the seven days from 6-12 July, according to the Fire and Disaster Management Agency.

Besides staying hydrated and turning on the air conditioning, some of the most basic actions repeatedly encouraged by government officials, residents have turned to new products and gadgets to beat the heat.

Items such as clothes with built-in fans, disposable wet-towels, UV and heat blocking umbrellas, and portable handheld fans have been popular and sold in shops nationwide.

Experts from the Japan Academic Network for Disaster Reduction are stressing the need for heat acclimatisation as it takes several weeks for the body to adapt to high temperatures.

They say the key is to start preparing for hot weather and taking measures to prevent heatstroke during the country's rainy season, before the full summer heat arrives.

Japan also faces high humidity which makes it harder for sweat to evaporate, reducing the body's ability to cool itself.

vocabulary.example: The company launched an initiative to reduce plastic waste.
The cramped seating caused discomfort for passengers on the long flight.
The advertisement was considered offensive to some viewers.
The unexpected result prompted a review of safety procedures.
The new film received mixed reviews from critics.
She was self-conscious about her body shape after gaining weight.
In some cultures, it is important to observe strict social etiquette during business meetings.
Many commuters opt for public transport to avoid traffic.
When faced with a difficult decision, he turned to his mentor for advice.
Mountain climbers need a period of acclimatisation to adjust to high altitudes.

difficult_sentences.sentence: Others say the policy is unfair to women since they are still expected to wear tights when showing a part of their legs.
difficult_sentences.explanation_zh: 主句为“Others say the policy is unfair to women”，后接since引导的原因状语从句，其中又包含when引导的时间状语从句（省略了主语和be动词，完整形式为“when they are showing”）。这种结构使句子紧凑，表达了政策对不同性别的差别影响。
difficult_sentences.grammar_point: 原因状语从句与时间状语从句的嵌套，非谓语省略
difficult_sentences.translation_zh: 另一些人说这项政策对女性不公平，因为在露出部分腿部时，她们仍被要求穿紧身袜。

difficult_sentences.sentence: He thinks women believe that 'men should have less leg hair', which has prompted them to seek laser hair removal so as not to offend those around them.
difficult_sentences.explanation_zh: 主句为“He thinks”，后接宾语从句“women believe...”，其中又包含that引导的宾语从句。关系代词which指代前面整个想法，引导非限制性定语从句，说明导致的结果；不定式短语“so as not to offend...”作目的状语。
difficult_sentences.grammar_point: 多层级宾语从句，非限制性定语从句，目的状语
difficult_sentences.translation_zh: 他认为女性相信“男性应该少些腿毛”，这促使她们寻求激光脱毛，以免冒犯周围的人。

difficult_sentences.sentence: As with other parts of the world, Japan has been experiencing record heat in recent months while grappling with soaring energy prices driven by instability in the Middle East.
difficult_sentences.explanation_zh: 句首“As with other parts of the world”为方式状语。主句使用现在完成进行时“has been experiencing”描述持续至今的状况。连词“while”引导省略主语的状语从句（while it is grappling）“while grappling...”，其中过去分词短语“driven by instability...”作定语修饰“energy prices”。
difficult_sentences.grammar_point: 现在完成进行时，while引导的省略主语状语从句，过去分词作定语
difficult_sentences.translation_zh: 与世界其他地区一样，日本近几个月来经历了创纪录的高温，同时还因中东动荡而面临飙升的能源价格。

difficult_sentences.sentence: Japan also faces high humidity which makes it harder for sweat to evaporate, reducing the body's ability to cool itself.
difficult_sentences.explanation_zh: 主句“Japan also faces high humidity”，which引导定语从句修饰humidity，其中“makes it harder”是“make + it + adj. + for...to do”结构。现在分词短语“reducing the body's ability...”作结果状语，表示前文情况导致的进一步后果。
difficult_sentences.grammar_point: 定语从句，make的不定式复合结构，现在分词作结果状语
difficult_sentences.translation_zh: 日本还面临高湿度，这使得汗液更难蒸发，从而降低了身体自我降温的能力。

idioms.usage_note: 用于非正式语境，指通过某种方式保持凉爽。常见搭配为“try to beat the heat”。
idioms.example: We set up a paddling pool in the garden to beat the heat during the summer.

idioms.usage_note: 口语表达，强调让更多人知道某事。可单独使用或后接“about something”。
idioms.example: The charity organized a social media campaign to get the word out about their donation drive.

idioms.usage_note: 因过分在意自己的外貌或行为而感到紧张或尴尬，常用“feel self-conscious about ...”。
idioms.example: He felt self-conscious about his accent when speaking in front of the class.

idioms.meaning_zh: 为社会所接受的；合乎社交礼仪的
idioms.usage_note: 描述行为、穿着等符合社会规范，不引起反感。常用于讨论新趋势或变化。
idioms.example: Working from home has become much more socially acceptable in recent years.

reading_questions.question: What is the central topic of the article?

options.A: The history of Japan's Cool Biz campaign
options.B: How Japanese women are protesting against leg hair harassment
options.C: The public reaction to Tokyo's shorts policy and the wider heat context
options.D: Energy-saving measures taken by the Tokyo government

reading_questions.question: According to the Gorilla Clinic survey, what percentage of respondents opposed wearing shorts to work?
What can be inferred from the phrase 'women may be more resistant to seeing a male colleague's bare legs'?

options.A: The shorts policy has caused particular unease among female employees
options.B: Women are more accepting of casual dress codes
options.C: Men are more self-conscious about their appearance
options.D: Men prefer to wear long trousers even in summer

reading_questions.question: In paragraph 8, the word 'prompted' most nearly means:
The author's tone towards the Tokyo shorts policy can best be described as:
How does the author structure the article?

options.A: Problem-solution format with the shorts policy as the solution to energy shortage
options.B: Introduction of the shorts policy and its mixed reception, followed by broader context of heat and energy issues
options.C: Chronological account of the Cool Biz campaign from 2005 to now
options.D: A comparison between men's and women's fashion choices in Japan

reading_questions.question: Which of the following would be the most appropriate title for this article?

options.A: Tokyo's Shorts Policy: A Complete Failure
options.B: How to Beat the Heat in Japan
options.C: Shorts in the Office: Tokyo's Controversial Energy-Saving Measure

sources.title: Tokyo urges men to wear shorts to work, but women say it's 'leg hair harassment'
